title: Language Teaching Application and Research Center - Regulations
content:
Language Teaching Application and Research Center - Regulations

ISTANBUL ESENYURT UNIVERSITY

REGULATION ON THE LANGUAGE TEACHING APPLICATION AND RESEARCH CENTER

CHAPTER ONE

Purpose, Scope, Legal Basis, and Definitions

Purpose

This Regulation has been prepared to regulate the procedures and principles regarding the purpose, fields of activity, administrative bodies, duties of the administrative bodies, and working methods of the Istanbul Esenyurt University Language Teaching Application and Research Center.

Scope

This Regulation covers the provisions regarding the fields of activity, administrative bodies, duties of the administrative bodies, and working methods of the Istanbul Esenyurt University Language Teaching Application and Research Center.

Legal Basis

This Regulation has been prepared based on subparagraph (2) of paragraph (d) of the first clause of Article 7 and Article 14 of the Higher Education Law No. 2547, dated 4/11/1981.

Definitions

The terms used in this Regulation refer to:

a)

Unit Supervisors

: Academic staff responsible for programming and implementing the Center’s activities,

b)

Advisory Board

: The Advisory Board of the Center,

c)

Administrative Coordinator

: The staff responsible for conducting the Center’s administrative affairs,

d)

Center

: Istanbul Esenyurt University Language Teaching Application and Research Center,

e)

Director

: The Director of the Center,

f)

Rector

: The Rector of Istanbul Esenyurt University,

g)

Senate

: The Senate of Istanbul Esenyurt University,

h)

University

: Istanbul Esenyurt University,

i)

Executive Board

: The Executive Board of the Center.

CHAPTER TWO

Purpose and Fields of Activity of the Center

Purpose of the Center

The purpose of the Center is to teach Turkish as a foreign or second language in a fluent, effective, and academic manner to international students at the University, foreign nationals coming to study in higher education institutions, visitors to our country, and anyone who wants to learn Turkish; to promote Turkey, Turkish history, and Turkish culture; to prepare necessary documentation and materials; to train teaching staff who will go abroad from Turkey to teach Turkish; to offer Turkish lessons and introduce Turkish culture and history to international visitors coming to Turkey and the University through international programs; to organize “Teaching Turkish to Foreigners” courses to enhance the skills and professional development of educators; to carry out joint project activities in cooperation with relevant national and international institutions and organizations; to conduct academic studies not only on the Turkish language but also on other foreign languages; to provide foreign language education and international experiences to both internal students/staff and external applicants; to organize trips and international programs to introduce students to the economies and cultures of Europe and other countries, to help them develop talents and social skills, and to support their social integration.

Fields of Activity of the Center

The Center’s fields of activity include:

a) Teaching Turkish and foreign languages both domestically and abroad; promoting Turkey, Turkish culture, and the culture and history of the taught languages; and establishing units for this purpose both in Turkey and abroad.

b) Opening courses, providing internships, and preparing informative guides about countries of assignment for academic staff and teachers appointed by relevant public institutions to teach Turkish domestically and internationally.

c) Providing foreign language training for international program participants to gain experience and internships in European countries. Conducting research and development for language teaching methods used in university departments teaching Turkish and foreign languages; organizing courses and seminars to share findings.

d) Conducting comparative studies between Turkish and foreign languages to enhance the effectiveness of teaching; developing relevant materials and assessment tools.

e) Organizing Turkish language courses for children of Turkish citizens living abroad.

f) Cooperating with universities and academic institutions abroad that teach Turkish or have relevant departments.

g) Teaching Turkish to students and academics arriving through inter-university cooperation protocols. Engaging in joint educational, research, implementation, and publication activities with university units and public institutions as per Turkey’s bilateral agreements.

h) Developing language teaching programs and methods, and cooperating with various institutions both in Turkey and abroad. Issuing certificates in accordance with the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFR).

i) Teaching Turkish to international students participating in EU-funded projects.

j) Offering Turkish and foreign language learning opportunities to prospective international students, academics, and staff of the University.

k) Organizing practical language teaching programs for senior students and graduates of Turkish Language Teaching and Turkish Language and Literature departments.

l) Providing Turkish and foreign language education to groups and individuals as per institutional protocols.

m) Organizing social and cultural activities, such as choirs, conversation clubs, and performances, to promote interaction between international students learning Turkish and Turkish students learning foreign languages.

n) Preparing and administering distance education programs and exams to promote Turkish language instruction; cooperating with international universities and language centers for student/faculty exchange and sharing of teaching resources.

CHAPTER THREE

Administrative Bodies and Their Duties

Administrative Bodies of the Center

The administrative bodies of the Center are:

a) The Director,

b) The Executive Board,

c) The Advisory Board.

Director

The Director is appointed by the Rector from among the full-time staff of the University for a term of three years. A deputy director is also appointed by the Rector upon the Director’s recommendation. The deputy director acts on behalf of the Director in their absence. If the Director is absent for more than six months, their term ends automatically.

Duties of the Director

The Director’s duties are:

a) To represent the Center,

b) To chair the Executive Board and implement its decisions,

c) To ensure regular operation, supervision, and inspection of the Center's activities,

d) To submit Executive Board decisions for the Rector’s approval,

e) To assign duties as required by the Center's work,

f) To convey needs and suggestions related to the Center's research and implementation activities to the Rector,

g) To submit an annual report on the Center’s operations to the Rector after consultation with the Executive Board,

h) To inform the Executive Board about the activities, plans, programs, and projects of established working groups,

i) To organize, manage, and supervise training, courses, workshops, congresses, certification programs, symposiums, and seminars,

j) To submit personnel recruitment and assignment proposals to the Rector.

(2)

Up to two deputy directors may be appointed by the Rector upon the Director’s recommendation. Their term ends with the Director’s term.

Executive Board

The Executive Board consists of five members: the Director, the deputy director, and three faculty members appointed by the Rector from related departments.

(2)

Board members serve for three years and may be reappointed. New members are appointed to complete the term of those who resign or are assigned outside the University for more than three months. The Board convenes at least twice a year upon the Director's call.

Duties of the Executive Board

The Executive Board is responsible for:

a) Making decisions regarding the management and activities of the Center,

b) Reviewing and approving the Center's annual academic calendar,

c) Evaluating the Director's personnel proposals and recommending them to the Rectorate,

d) Organizing in-service training programs for academic staff,

e) Determining the annual student intake,

f) Providing opinions on research, publications, and educational activities,

g) Reviewing the Director’s annual report and preparing the following year's work plan,

h) Recommending tuition fees for Center branches to the Rector,

i) Recommending the opening or closing of Center branches to the Rector,

j) Setting the principles for new branches and submitting them to the University Administrative Board.

Advisory Board and Its Duties

The Advisory Board consists of up to ten individuals who have worked or are working in relevant fields. Members are appointed by the Rector for a term of three years upon the Director’s recommendation. Members may be from inside or outside the University.

(2)

The Advisory Board convenes when deemed necessary by the Executive Board on a date determined by the Director.

(3)

The duty of the Advisory Board is to provide suggestions and contributions regarding the Center’s activities.

CHAPTER FOUR

Miscellaneous and Final Provisions

Cases Not Regulated

In cases not covered by this Regulation, the relevant legislation and decisions of the Senate and the University Executive Board shall apply.

Personnel Requirements

The academic, technical, and administrative personnel needs of the Center are met by University staff assigned by the Rector under Article 13 of Law No. 2547 upon the Director’s proposal, or by personnel employed under the provisions of the Labor Law No. 4857 dated 22/5/2003.

Enforcement

This Regulation enters into force on the date of its publication.

Execution

The provisions of this Regulation are executed by the Rector of Istanbul Esenyurt University.
